expect: A turnkey property solution: multi-language landings, multi-currency pricing, full property catalogue with filters and Google Maps, mortgage calculator. No more than 5 hours of your time. Live in 6-10 weeks, and you take leads from *5 markets at once*.
to: A turnkey property solution: English-first landings, GBP pricing with overseas currencies on request, a full property catalogue with filters and Google Maps, and a mortgage calculator. No more than 5 hours of your time. Live in 6-10 weeks, and you take leads from *the UK and overseas at once*.

expect: The client is looking for a 2-3 bedroom apartment in the €150-200k range in the Born district of Barcelona with parking. On your site — 200 listings in a feed without filters by area, m², budget.
to: The client is looking for a 2-3 bedroom flat in the £250-350k range in a particular part of Manchester with parking. On your site — 200 listings in a feed without filters by area, m², budget.
why: Rules 6 & 7: GBP not EUR; reframe Barcelona example to a UK town for the UK reader; flat not apartment per style guide.

why: Rules 5 & 6: strip Ukraine-specific and post-Soviet language framing; reframe generically for the UK reader with English-first.

expect: We were looking for a turnkey solution for our agency — not to build from scratch. Code-Site.Art used Your House Albania as the reference, customised it to our market in 4 weeks. We launched with three languages and two currencies on day one.
to: We were looking for a turnkey solution for our agency, not to build from scratch. Code-Site.Art used Your House Albania as the reference and customised it to our market in 4 weeks. We launched in English with two currencies on day one.

expect: Depends on the country. US/Canada — RETS / RESO Web API standard, connected via official AOR accounts. Europe — fragmented (Idealista, Rightmove, ImmoScout24 — each with its own API). Each integration — £1,200-2,500. If you have no MLS — manual entries via Sanity (5 min per property).
to: Depends on the portal. In the UK, listings flow through Rightmove and Zoopla feeds, connected via their official accounts. OnTheMarket adds a third route. Each integration runs £1,200-2,500. If you've no feed in place, you add properties manually via Sanity (5 min per property).
why: Rule 1: RETS/RESO (US feeds) → Rightmove/Zoopla feeds; Idealista→Rightmove, ImmoScout24→Zoopla, OnTheMarket as the distinct third portal; make body consistent with SEO meta.

expect: Local bank rates are stored in Sanity and updated via central bank APIs (UA: NBU, EU: ECB, US: Fed). The calculator picks rates by user geo-location or selected region. You can optionally configure "our rates" if you have partnerships with specific banks.
to: Local bank rates are stored in Sanity and updated via central bank data (the Bank of England base rate, plus the ECB for euro-area markets). The calculator picks rates by user geo-location or selected region. You can optionally configure "our rates" if you have partnerships with specific lenders.
why: Rule 3: NBU → Bank of England, drop Fed; ECB kept for genuine euro-area context (Albania/Italy buyers); 'lenders' reads UK.

expect: Technically yes, but legally complex. Reservation via deposit (£1,000-5,000 via Stripe / LiqPay) as intent confirmation — standard. Full transaction — through notary or escrow service, not the site. DocuSign for preliminary contracts. If you’re an online-only agency — we wire the full funnel.
to: Technically yes, but legally complex. Taking a reservation deposit (£1,000-5,000 via Stripe or GoCardless) to confirm intent is standard. The full transaction runs through a solicitor or escrow, not the site. DocuSign handles preliminary contracts. If you're an online-only agency, we wire up the full funnel.
why: Rule 2: LiqPay → GoCardless, keep Stripe; 'solicitor' is the UK term (style guide §3); fewer em dashes per voice rules.

expect: KeyCRM
to: Street.co.uk
why: KeyCRM->UK estate-agency CRM (distinct from Reapit/Alto)
